task_description: Evaluate the interestingness scores against a simple frequency baseline (count only) and discuss whether the normalisation step adds measurable value.
adaptation_notes: LLM suggested the comparison methodology (Spearman rank correlation between baseline and interestingness-ranked lists) and helped identify appropriate test phrases. Implementation was independently developed.
top_5_prompts: How would you compare a pure frequency baseline against the interestingness scoring formula to evaluate whether normalisation adds value?
Compute Spearman rank correlation between frequency-ranked and interestingness-ranked phrase lists and interpret the result.
What kinds of phrases would rank identically under both methods and what kinds of phrases would diverge significantly?
Design an experiment to test whether the interestingness score outperforms a simple frequency count at identifying manually labelled trending phrases.
What is a realistic null hypothesis for whether the power-law exponent matters, and how would you test it statistically?
